evidence: C1 Flag model on Plan. Net-new plans/models/flag.py: Flag FK Plan (CASCADE, related_name flags), element_path, message, created_by_spec, created_at (auto_now_add), verdict (nullable, unconstrained), severity (FlagSeverity TextChoices must-fix/should-review/informational in plans/choices.py), is_resolved (default False); re-exported in plans/models/__init__. Migration 0048_flag (dep 0047) applied to the dev DB. New plans/tests/test_flag.py (10 tests). Separate-agent adversarial audit Total findings: 0 (independent probes: CASCADE delete, nullable verdict, severity full_clean rejection; noqa DJ001 matches verification.py:78 + action.py:744 precedents; message correctly not in translation.py; scope clean). Full gate GREEN: makemigrations --check clean, ruff check + format, mypy . 317/0, pytest 1991, make test-js 42/0; showmigrations shows 0048_flag [X].

criteria_results.criterion: A plan can carry any number of persisted flags, and they remain retrievable after a reload.
Each flag records the element it addresses, a message, the spec that produced it, its creation time, its verdict, and whether it is resolved (unresolved by default).
A flag's severity is exactly one of must-fix, should-review, or informational; any other value is rejected.
Deleting a plan deletes its flags; no flag outlives its plan.
The full project gate stays green.
